source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, it is known that the length of the fishing rod $AC$ is $10\mathrm{m}$, and the length of the fishing line $BC$ exposed above the water surface is $6\mathrm{m}$. A fisher wants to check the situation on the hook, so he rotates the fishing rod $AC$ to the position $A{C}^{\prime}$, at this time, the length of the fishing line ${B}^{\prime}{C}^{\prime}$ exposed above the water surface is $8\mathrm{m}$. What is the length of $B{B}^{\prime}$?

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says "As shown in the figure"
the solution uses diagram-derived right angles and positional relations (e.g. \u2220ABC=90\u00b0, \u2220AB'C'=90\u00b0, and BB'=AB-AB') that are not fully specified in text alone
multi_step: the provided solution performs two separate Pythagorean computations: first AB from AC and BC, then AB' from AC' and B'C'
a final comparison/subtraction step is required to obtain BB'
solving combines visual geometric interpretation with arithmetic rather than a single direct lookup
verifiability: the target answer is a single numeric length (2 m), which is easy to check
the answer_text gives an explicit deterministic derivation with concrete intermediate values 8 and 6
registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true support reliable downstream checking

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和关键配图均已齐全，核心语义资产完整
初步打分较高，图像依赖性强但当前图像文件存在，可支撑理解与复核
答案为可验证的确定性数值结果，且提供了明确推导过程
虽有“几何关系未完全由文本显式给出”的风险标记，但该风险已由现有配图覆盖，不构成核心语义缺失

normalization_record.normalized_question_text: As shown in the figure, it is known that the length of the fishing rod $AC$ is $10\mathrm{m}$, and the length of the fishing line $BC$ exposed above the water surface is $6\mathrm{m}$. A fisher wants to check the situation on the hook, so he rotates the fishing rod $AC$ to the position $AC'$, at this time, the length of the fishing line $B'C'$ exposed above the water surface is $8\mathrm{m}$. What is the length of $BB'$?
normalization_record.normalized_answer_text: \textbf{Solution:} Given $AC=10\text{ m}$, $BC=6\text{ m}$, and $\angle ABC=90^\circ$, therefore $AB=\sqrt{AC^2-BC^2}=\sqrt{10^2-6^2}=8\text{ m}$. Given $AC'=10\text{ m}$, $B'C'=8\text{ m}$, and $\angle AB'C'=90^\circ$, therefore $AB'=\sqrt{AC'^2-B'C'^2}=\sqrt{10^2-8^2}=6\text{ m}$. Therefore $BB'=AB-AB'=\boxed{2}\text{ m}$.
normalization_notes: Retained the explicit figure reference because the geometry setup depends on the diagram.
Normalized prime notation and spacing in the math expressions without changing the original meaning.
